title: IRS Form 15620 Section 83(b) Election — Fill Library
transferor_role: person performing services (founder/employee/contractor) who received restricted stock

deadline.rule: file no later than 30 days after the transfer date (Box 3)

questionnaire.ask: Full legal name of the transferor?
Mailing address (street, city, state/province, ZIP/postal, country)?
Number of shares, class, and company?
Transfer date (RSPA signed + shares issued)? = Day 0
Your taxable year containing the transfer date?
Fair market value per share at transfer (ignore lapse restrictions)?

box2.label: Property + quantity
box2.example: 8,000,000 shares of Common Stock of Example Holdings, Inc.

box5.label: Restrictions
box5.example: 4-yr vesting, 1-yr cliff; unvested shares forfeited at cost if service ends

box6.label: FMV at transfer
box6.note: FMV determined without regard to lapse restrictions (Treas. Reg. 1.83-3(i))

box8.label: Amount to include in gross income
box8.note: approximately 0 for founder stock at formation

example_filled_roof10x_founder.box2: 8,000,000 shares of Common Stock of Example Holdings, Inc.
example_filled_roof10x_founder.box5: Shares subject to a Company repurchase option at cost lapsing over a 4-year vesting schedule (1-year cliff, then monthly); unvested shares forfeited on termination of service.

filing_workflow: 1. Confirm Box 3 transfer date; compute 30-day deadline (exclude Day 0; rollover weekends/holidays).
2. Complete + sign Form 15620 from fields above.
3. File ONE way: electronic via IRS portal (ID.me, allow 1-2 days) OR paper certified mail to your IRS service center.
4. Post-Dec-24-2025: hand paper to USPS clerk for same-day postmark; no collection boxes.
5. Deliver a copy to the company (required).
6. Keep copy + proof (e-file confirmation or certified receipt) indefinitely.
7. Log in cap table + corporate data room.
